title: huperzia hystrix
intro: Huperzia hystrix, also known as the forked polypody or forked huperzia, is a species of fern in the family Huperziaceae. It is a vascular plant that belongs to the division Lycopodiophyta, which is a group of ancient, non-vascular plants that have adapted to modern environments over time. The medicinal properties of Huperzia hystrix are attributed to its high concentration of huperzine A, a natural alkaloid with neuroprotective and anti-inflammatory properties, which has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. Huperzine A is often extracted from the plant and used in herbal preparations, such as capsules or teas, to support cognitive function, memory, and overall brain health.
